name: Spicy Green Beans (Masaledar Sem)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the green beans and cut them into crosswise pieces about 1/4 inch thick.
2. Place the ginger and garlic in an electric blender or food processor. Add 1/3 of the water and blend until smooth.
3. Heat the oil in a large, heavy saucepan over medium heat.
4. When hot, add the cumin seeds. Five seconds later, add the crushed chili.
5. As soon as the cumin seeds darken, pour in the ginger-garlic paste.
6. Stir gently and cook for about 1 minute.
7. Add the coriander. Stir a few times.
8. Add the chopped tomatoes. Stir and cook until just tender, about 2 minutes, mashing up the tomato pieces with the back of a slotted spoon as you do so.
9. Add the beans, salt, and remaining water. Bring to a simmer.
10. Cover and turn the heat down to low. Cook for about 8-10 minutes or until the beans are tender.
11. Remove the cover. Stir in the lemon juice, roasted cumin, and a generous amount of freshly ground pepper.
12. Increase the heat to medium-high and boil away all of the liquid, stirring occasionally until reduced.
